steps.title: Substitute the given value x = 2 into the equation
steps.content:
We're given $\frac{1}{cx} = \frac{x}{72} + \frac{1}{c}$ and solving for $c$ when $x = 2$. Substituting $x = 2$:

$$\frac{1}{c(2)} = \frac{2}{72} + \frac{1}{c}$$

$$\frac{1}{2c} = \frac{1}{36} + \frac{1}{c}$$

where we simplified $\frac{2}{72} = \frac{1}{36}$.

steps.title: Isolate all terms containing c on the left side
steps.content:
Subtract $\frac{1}{c}$ from both sides:

$$\frac{1}{2c} - \frac{1}{c} = \frac{1}{36}$$

To combine the left side, find a common denominator of $2c$:

$$\frac{1}{2c} - \frac{2}{2c} = \frac{1}{36}$$

$$\frac{-1}{2c} = \frac{1}{36}$$

steps.title: Cross-multiply and solve for c
steps.content:
From $\frac{-1}{2c} = \frac{1}{36}$, cross-multiply:

$$-1 \cdot 36 = 2c \cdot 1$$

$$-36 = 2c$$

$$c = -18$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
